title: C2PA Conformance Predicates v2.4
description: Deterministic, machine-checkable predicates translated from C2PA v2.4 normative validation rules. Each predicate formalizes one or more SHALL/MUST rules into structured conditions that a conformance test harness can evaluate without human judgment.

text_plain.description: Unstructured text assets with C2PATextManifestWrapper embedding

predicates.title: Text wrapper presence and extraction
predicates.description: For unstructured text with an embedded C2PATextManifestWrapper, the validator shall locate the wrapper, extract the manifest reference, and separate the wrapper bytes from the content bytes before computing the data hash.

input.asset_bytes: The complete byte content of the text file
input.data_hash_assertion: The c2pa.hash.data assertion from the active manifest

steps.description: Scan the asset bytes for the C2PATextManifestWrapper magic number
Parse the located wrapper structure to extract the manifest URI or inline data
Verify that exactly one valid C2PATextManifestWrapper exists in the asset
Compute the hash over the text content bytes with the wrapper bytes treated as an exclusion range, using the algorithm specified in the data hash assertion's alg field
Compare the computed hash against the hash field in the data hash assertion

passing.description: A text file with a single valid C2PATextManifestWrapper whose data hash matches after excluding wrapper bytes
passing.notes:
Hex-encoded: 'Hello World
C2PA_MANIFEST...
'. The wrapper region is the C2PA_MANIFEST line. The hash is computed over 'Hello World
' only.

failing_no_wrapper.description: A text file with no C2PATextManifestWrapper present

failing_multiple_wrappers.description: A text file containing two valid C2PATextManifestWrapper blocks

failing_hash_mismatch.description: A text file where the content has been modified after signing, so the hash no longer matches

predicates.title: Hash algorithm validation for text data hash
predicates.description: The hash algorithm specified in the data hash assertion's alg field must appear in the allowed or deprecated algorithm list.

input.alg: The algorithm identifier from the data hash assertion

image.description: Static image assets bound via byte-range data hash

predicates.title: Exclusion range ordering and non-negativity
predicates.description: Exclusion ranges in the data hash assertion must be non-overlapping and sorted by start offset. All start and length values must be non-negative.

input.exclusions: Array of {start, length} from the DataHashMap

failing_overlap.reason: First range ends at 250 which exceeds second range start of 200

predicates.title: Exclusion range within asset bounds
predicates.description: No exclusion range may extend beyond the end of the asset. If start + length exceeds the asset size, the manifest is rejected.

input.exclusions: Array of {start, length} from the DataHashMap
input.asset_size: Total byte length of the asset

failing.reason: Exclusion range end (2100) exceeds asset size (1024)

predicates.title: Data hash computation and match
predicates.description: The hash is computed over all asset bytes except those in exclusion ranges. The hash field must be present, and the computed hash must match the declared hash value.

input.asset_bytes: The complete byte content of the asset
input.data_hash_assertion: The DataHashMap from the active manifest

passing.description: Asset bytes hashed with SHA-256 excluding the manifest store region, hash matches declared value

failing_tampered_content.description: One byte of image content modified after signing

predicates.title: Exclusion range content restrictions
predicates.description: Exclusion ranges must contain only the C2PA Manifest Store and appropriate padding (zero bytes or format-specific padding). Any other data in exclusion ranges causes rejection. Additional exclusion ranges beyond the manifest store trigger an informational code.

input.exclusions: Array of {start, length} from the DataHashMap
input.asset_bytes: The complete byte content of the asset

passing.description: Single exclusion range containing only the C2PA Manifest Store

informational_extra_exclusion.description: Two exclusion ranges: one for manifest store, one for padding

failing_unexpected_data.description: Exclusion range contains non-zero, non-manifest-store data

video_bmff.description: BMFF-based video and audio assets (MP4, MOV, M4A, 3GP) bound via box-level hashing

predicates.title: BMFF exclusions field presence
predicates.description: The bmff-hash-map must contain an exclusions field whose value is an array with at least one entry.

input.bmff_hash_map: The BmffHashMap from the active manifest's c2pa.hash.bmff assertion

predicates.title: BMFF subset range ordering and non-negativity
predicates.description: Subset ranges within the BMFF hash must be non-overlapping and sorted. All offset and length values must be non-negative.

input.subset_ranges: Array of {offset, length} from the BMFF hash subset definition

failing_overlap.reason: First range ends at 700 which exceeds second range offset of 600

predicates.title: BMFF Merkle tree validation
predicates.description: When the merkle field is present, the Merkle tree structure must be internally consistent: block sizes must account for the full mdat payload, fixedBlockSize and variableBlockSizes are mutually exclusive, leaf hashes must match, and the tree root must match the declared hash.

input.mdat_payload: The raw bytes of the mdat box payload

steps.description: Sum of block sizes must equal the mdat payload size
merkle_map.count must equal the number of elements in merkle_map.hashes
Hash each block of mdat and compare to corresponding leaf hash
Compute the Merkle tree root from leaf hashes and compare to declared hash

passing.description: 4 blocks of mdat, all leaf hashes match, Merkle root matches declared hash

failing_both_block_fields.reason: fixedBlockSize and variableBlockSizes are mutually exclusive

failing_leaf_mismatch.description: One block has been modified after signing

predicates.title: BMFF additional exclusions informational
predicates.description: If a validator encounters exclusion ranges other than the required C2PA exclusions, free boxes, or skip boxes, an informational code is issued.

input.exclusions: Array of exclusion entries from BmffHashMap

predicates.title: BMFF hash match and multi-asset fallback
predicates.description: If BMFF hash validation succeeds, assertion.bmffHash.match is recorded; on failure the validator must check for a multi-asset hash assertion and validate it instead before issuing assertion.bmffHash.mismatch.

then.note: Do not issue assertion.bmffHash.mismatch if multi-asset validates

passing.description: BMFF hash computed over all relevant boxes matches the declared hash

failing_reason.description: BMFF hash mismatches and no multi-asset assertion is present

predicates.title: BMFF Merkle last block boundary clamping
predicates.description: If the final Merkle tree block extends beyond the end of the mdat payload, its size should be clamped to end at the payload boundary.

then.description: Clamp last block size to mdat payload end

passing.description: Final Merkle block is clamped to the mdat payload end offset and hash matches

failing_reason.description: Final Merkle block extends past mdat payload and is not clamped, causing hash mismatch

predicates.title: Non-fragmented BMFF Merkle tree leaf hash validation
predicates.description: For a non-fragmented asset using a Merkle tree, each auxiliary uuid C2PA box leaf hash must match the corresponding element in merkle-map.hashes; a hash mismatch rejects with assertion.bmffHash.mismatch and a merkle-map.count smaller than the leaf count rejects with assertion.bmffHash.malformed.

passing.description: All leaf node hashes match their corresponding entries in merkle-map.hashes and count is correct

failing_reason.description: One auxiliary uuid box has been modified, causing its leaf hash to not match merkle-map.hashes

predicates.title: Fragmented BMFF Merkle tree validation
predicates.description: When bmff-hash-map.merkle is present on a fragmented asset, the validator must validate the Merkle tree; if the auxiliary uuid C2PA box is absent the manifest is rejected with assertion.bmffHash.malformed, and leaf hash mismatches cause assertion.bmffHash.mismatch.

passing.description: Fragmented asset with bmff-hash-map.merkle present; all leaf hashes match and auxiliary uuid box exists

failing_reason.description: bmff-hash-map.merkle is present but the auxiliary uuid C2PA box is absent

audio_wav.description: Non-BMFF audio assets (WAV, FLAC, AIFF) bound via byte-range data hash. Uses the same DataHashMap mechanism as images.

predicates.title: Audio data hash validation (non-BMFF)
predicates.description: Non-BMFF audio formats use the same byte-range data hash mechanism as images. The validator computes a hash over all asset bytes except exclusion ranges (containing the C2PA Manifest Store) and compares against the declared hash. All predicates from PRED-IMG-001 through PRED-IMG-004 apply identically.

input.asset_bytes: The complete byte content of the audio file
input.data_hash_assertion: The DataHashMap from the active manifest

condition.note: Non-BMFF audio uses identical data hash validation as images. The format-specific difference is only in where the C2PA Manifest Store is embedded (RIFF chunk for WAV, metadata block for FLAC).

passing.description: WAV file with C2PA manifest in a RIFF chunk, data hash matches after excluding the manifest chunk

failing.description: WAV file with modified audio samples after signing

predicates.title: Rendered audio content coverage
predicates.description: For any portion of an asset rendered for presentation to a user, including audio, the hard binding must cover those bytes. Portions not covered by the binding cannot be validated.

input.rendered_byte_ranges: Byte ranges of the asset that are rendered to the user
input.exclusions: Exclusion ranges from the hash assertion
input.asset_size: Total byte length of the asset

condition.description: The set of bytes hashed (asset_size minus exclusion ranges) must be a superset of the bytes rendered to the user

on_uncovered_rendered_bytes.note: Content rendered to the user was not covered by the hash binding

passing.description: All rendered audio samples fall within hashed byte ranges

failing.description: Audio samples in a rendered region fall within an exclusion range

document_pdf.description: PDF documents with incremental-update manifest embedding

predicates.title: Single manifest store per PDF update section
predicates.description: A PDF file may contain multiple C2PA Manifest Stores across incremental update sections, but each update section shall contain at most one C2PA Manifest Store.

input.pdf_update_sections: Parsed list of PDF incremental update sections with their embedded manifest stores

on_violation.note: More than one C2PA Manifest Store in a single PDF update section

passing.description: PDF with two incremental updates, each containing zero or one manifest store

failing.description: PDF with one update section containing two manifest stores

predicates.title: Earlier PDF manifest stores remain valid
predicates.description: C2PA Manifest Stores present in earlier incremental updates of a PDF, or in the original PDF body, shall be considered valid and processed accordingly. A validator must not discard or ignore them.

input.pdf_update_sections: Ordered list of PDF incremental update sections with manifest stores
input.active_manifest_section: Index of the section containing the active (latest) manifest

check.description: Each earlier manifest store is parsed and validated as a complete C2PA Manifest Store

on_skip.note: Validator ignored a valid manifest store from an earlier update section

passing.description: PDF with original body manifest and one incremental update manifest, both validated

failing_skipped_earlier_store.description: Validator skips the original body manifest and only reads the latest update section
failing_skipped_earlier_store.reason: Earlier manifest stores must be validated, not discarded

predicates.title: PDF update manifest exclusion range adjustment
predicates.description: When update manifests are present, the exclusion range covering the C2PA Manifest Store must use the current total length of the manifest store (not the original). Subsequent exclusion ranges must have their start offsets adjusted to account for the shifted content.

input.exclusions: Array of {start, length} from the DataHashMap
input.manifest_store_offset: Byte offset of the C2PA Manifest Store
input.manifest_store_current_length: Current total byte length of the manifest store after updates

then.description: The exclusion range at manifest_store_offset must have length equal to manifest_store_current_length
All exclusion ranges after the manifest store must have their start values incremented by the difference between current and original manifest store sizes

passing.description: Exclusion ranges properly adjusted for a PDF with one update manifest that grew the manifest store by 500 bytes

failing.description: Exclusion range uses the original manifest store length instead of the current length

multi_asset.description: Compound assets composed of multiple separately-hashed parts (e.g., HTML with embedded media)

predicates.title: Parts field presence and non-empty
predicates.description: The multi-asset-hash-map must contain a parts field that is a non-empty array.

input.multi_asset_hash_map: The MultiAssetHashMap from the active manifest

predicates.title: Part locator and hash assertion presence
predicates.description: Each part in the multi-asset hash must contain both a valid locator and a valid hashAssertion field.

predicates.title: Part coverage completeness
predicates.description: The located parts must not overlap and must, in aggregate, cover every byte of the asset.

input.located_parts: Array of resolved byte ranges for each part
input.asset_size: Total byte length of the asset

checks.description: No two parts may overlap in byte ranges
Union of all part byte ranges must equal [0, asset_size)

failing_gap.reason: Bytes 400-599 not covered by any part

predicates.title: Multi-asset hash parts iteration
predicates.description: Validation of c2pa.hash.multi-asset must proceed by iterating over every element of the parts array in the multi-asset-hash-map.

passing.description: All parts in the multi-asset-hash-map locate and validate successfully

failing_reason.description: One part in the array fails to locate, causing assertion.multiAssetHash.missingPart

predicates.title: Multi-asset part locator validity
predicates.description: For a byte-offset-locator, byteOffset and length must be present and non-negative; for a bmffBox locator, the specified box must exist; violations in either case cause assertion.multiAssetHash.malformed.

passing.description: Part uses a byte-offset-locator with non-negative byteOffset and length; referenced range exists

failing_reason.description: Part uses a byte-offset-locator but the length field is absent

predicates.title: Multi-asset part location and optional handling
predicates.description: The validator must attempt to locate each part using its locator; if the part is absent and optional is false or absent, the manifest is rejected with assertion.multiAssetHash.missingPart; if optional is true, the part is skipped.

then.description: Part is optional and absent; skip it

passing.description: Part is absent but its optional field is true; validator skips it without error

failing_reason.description: Part is absent and optional field is false

predicates.title: Multi-asset part hash computation and match
predicates.description: For each located part, the validator computes the hash using the part's algorithm and methodology, treating absolute byte offsets as relative to the part start; a truncated part or hash mismatch causes assertion.multiAssetHash.mismatch, and all parts passing records assertion.multiAssetHash.match with no hard-binding failure codes.

steps.note: Absolute byte offsets in the hash assertion are relative to the part start
Do not record any hard-binding failure codes when multi-asset hash succeeds

passing.description: All located parts hash correctly and their lengths are not truncated

failing_reason.description: One part's actual byte length is shorter than the locator-declared length

boxes_hash.description: Box-based asset formats bound via general box hashing. Covers JPEG XL, fonts (TrueType/OpenType/WOFF2), and JPEG APP11 segments.

predicates.title: Boxes array field presence
predicates.description: The boxes hash assertion must contain a boxes field. The validator extracts the list of boxes to validate from this field.

input.boxes_hash_assertion: The boxes hash assertion from the active manifest

predicates.title: Box ordering matches asset
predicates.description: The boxes in the asset must appear in the same order as they appear in the boxes array of the assertion, including the box containing the C2PA Manifest Store. Out-of-order boxes cause rejection.

input.boxes_in_assertion: Ordered array of box identifiers from the boxes hash assertion
input.boxes_in_asset: Ordered array of box identifiers as they appear in the asset file

failing_reordered.reason: Boxes appear in different order than declared

predicates.title: No unexpected boxes in asset
predicates.description: If there are any boxes present in the asset that are not listed in the boxes array, the manifest is rejected. Every box in the asset must be accounted for.

input.boxes_in_assertion: Set of box identifiers from the boxes hash assertion
input.boxes_in_asset: Set of box identifiers present in the asset

failing_extra_box.reason: Asset contains 'jxlp' box not listed in assertion

predicates.title: Names field presence in box-hash-map
predicates.description: Each box-hash-map entry in the boxes array must contain a names field identifying which boxes it covers.

input.boxes_array: Array of box-hash-map entries from the assertion

predicates.title: Box exclusion range ordering and non-negativity
predicates.description: Within each box-hash-map, exclusion ranges must be non-overlapping with monotonically increasing start values. All start and length values must be non-negative.

input.exclusions: Array of {start, length} from a box-hash-map entry

condition.note: Box exclusion range validation follows the same logic as data hash exclusion ranges.

predicates.title: Exclusion range within box bounds
predicates.description: No exclusion range within a box may extend beyond the end of that box. This is analogous to PRED-IMG-002 but scoped to the box, not the entire asset.

input.exclusions: Array of {start, length} from a box-hash-map entry
input.box_size: Byte length of the box containing these exclusions

predicates.title: Per-box hash computation and match
predicates.description: For each box-hash-map entry, the hash is computed over the box bytes (excluding exclusion ranges and pad/pad2 fields). If names contains multiple entries, the hash covers the contiguous range from the start of the first named box to the end of the last, including any bytes between them. The computed hash must match the declared hash field.

input.box_hash_map: A single box-hash-map entry with names, alg, hash, exclusions
input.asset_bytes: The complete byte content of the asset

steps.description: Determine the byte range to hash: if names has one entry, hash that box; if multiple, hash from start of first to end of last including inter-box bytes

steps.data: resolved byte range minus exclusion ranges minus pad/pad2 bytes

passing.description: Single box with SHA-256 hash matching after exclusions

failing_no_hash.description: box-hash-map entry with hash field missing

predicates.title: Non-excluded box hash mismatch is fatal
predicates.description: If the hash for any box does not match and that box does not have an excluded field with a value of true, the manifest is rejected. Boxes marked excluded=true are ignored during hash comparison.

input.box_hash_results: Array of {box_name, hash_matches, excluded} for each box

predicates.title: JPEG APP11 segment coverage
predicates.description: If an APP11 marker segment that is not part of the C2PA Manifest Store is present in a JPEG asset and is not included in the list of hashed boxes, the manifest is rejected. All non-manifest APP11 segments must be explicitly listed.

input.app11_segments_in_asset: List of APP11 segments found in the JPEG asset
input.manifest_store_app11_segments: APP11 segments that are part of the C2PA Manifest Store
input.boxes_in_assertion: Box identifiers listed in the boxes hash assertion

passing.description: All APP11 segments either belong to the manifest store or are listed in the boxes hash

failing.description: JPEG has an APP11 XMP segment not listed in boxes hash and not part of manifest store

predicates.title: Font table coverage
predicates.description: For font assets, if any font tables are present that are not covered by any box in the boxes hash assertion, the manifest is rejected.

input.font_tables_in_asset: Set of font table tags present in the font file
input.tables_covered_by_boxes: Set of font table tags covered by the boxes hash assertion entries

failing_uncovered_table.reason: DSIG table not covered by any box hash entry

predicates.title: Box exclusion range content restrictions
predicates.description: Exclusion ranges in box hashes must contain only the C2PA Manifest Store and appropriate padding. Any other data causes rejection. Additional exclusion ranges beyond the manifest store trigger an informational code.

input.exclusions: Array of exclusion entries from box-hash-map
input.asset_bytes: The complete byte content of the asset

condition.note: Box exclusion content validation follows the same logic as data hash exclusion content validation, scoped to the box level.

passing.description: Single exclusion range containing the C2PA Manifest Store

informational_extra_exclusion.description: Additional exclusion range beyond the manifest store

failing_unexpected_data.description: Exclusion range contains non-zero, non-manifest-store data

predicates.title: Box index existence check
predicates.description: If the box indicated by boxIndex does not exist in the asset, the manifest must be rejected with assertion.boxesHash.malformed.

passing.description: boxIndex refers to a box that is present in the asset

failing_reason.description: boxIndex value exceeds the number of boxes in the asset

predicates.title: Boxes hash exclusion computation
predicates.description: The hash for a general box hash assertion must be computed over all asset bytes except those covered by the exclusions field and any box with excluded set to true.

checks.description: Byte ranges specified in exclusions field
All boxes where the excluded field is true

passing.description: Hash computed over non-excluded bytes matches the declared hash value

failing_reason.description: Excluded bytes are not omitted from the hash computation, producing a mismatch

predicates.title: Exclusion range padding security note
predicates.description: Combinations of exclusion ranges and padding may allow an attacker to substitute arbitrary data within the padding without invalidating the hash; validators should be aware of this risk.

condition.description: Informational check: flag assets with large exclusion ranges that include padding bytes beyond the manifest store

passing.description: Exclusion range contains only the manifest store with no additional mutable padding

failing_reason.description: Exclusion range is significantly larger than the manifest store, leaving unrestricted padding bytes

predicates.title: Boxes hash exclusion range manifest store integrity
predicates.description: For exclusion ranges containing a C2PA Manifest Store, the validator must confirm the range contains only the manifest store and appropriate zero or free/skip-box padding.

passing.description: Exclusion range for manifest store contains only the manifest and a trailing free box

failing_reason.description: Exclusion range contains unexpected non-padding data after the manifest store

predicates.title: Boxes hash metadata and inter-box bytes scope
predicates.description: Exclusion ranges beyond the manifest store may include asset metadata bytes, and the hash computation may cover arbitrary bytes between boxes.

condition.description: Advisory: account for metadata bytes in non-manifest-store exclusions and inter-box bytes when computing hash

passing.description: Hash computed correctly accounts for all non-excluded bytes including inter-box bytes

failing_reason.description: Inter-box bytes are omitted from the hash computation, causing a mismatch

predicates.title: Boxes hash multi-asset fallback
predicates.description: If box hash validation produces assertion.boxesHash.mismatch, the validator must check for a multi-asset hash assertion; if present it must be validated, otherwise the manifest is rejected with assertion.boxesHash.mismatch.

passing.description: Box hash fails but a multi-asset hash assertion is present and validates successfully

failing_reason.description: Box hash fails and no multi-asset hash assertion is present

collection_hash.description: Collection assets (ZIP archives, directory bundles) bound via per-URI hashing

predicates.title: URI and hash field presence
predicates.description: Each uri-hashed-data-map entry in the uris array must contain both a uri field and a hash field.

input.uris_array: Array of uri-hashed-data-map entries from the collection hash assertion

predicates.title: URI security validation
predicates.description: URIs must not contain '..' (parent directory traversal) or '.' (current directory) path components. These are rejected to prevent path traversal attacks.

input.uris: Array of URI strings from the collection hash assertion

check.description: URI must not contain parent directory traversal or current directory references

predicates.title: Per-URI hash computation and match
predicates.description: For each URI, the validator retrieves the asset and computes the hash over all bytes using the specified algorithm. The computed hash must match the declared hash.

input.asset_bytes: The retrieved bytes for this URI

steps.data: all bytes of the asset retrieved from the URI

passing.description: SHA-256 hash of retrieved file matches declared hash

failing_tampered.description: File has been modified after collection was signed

predicates.title: Collection file count validation
predicates.description: If any files listed in the collection hash assertion are not found by the validator, the manifest is rejected. All declared URIs must be resolvable.

input.declared_uris: Set of URIs from the collection hash assertion
input.found_uris: Set of URIs the validator was able to resolve

failing_missing_file.reason: c.mp3 listed in assertion but not found in collection

predicates.title: ZIP central directory hash
predicates.description: When validating a ZIP file, the zip_central_directory_hash field must be present and its hash must match the computed hash of the ZIP Central Directory.

input.zip_central_directory_bytes: The raw bytes of the ZIP Central Directory

passing.description: ZIP central directory hash matches

failing_hash_mismatch.description: ZIP central directory has been modified (files added/removed after signing)

structured_text.description: Structured text formats (SVG, XHTML, XML) with inline manifest blocks delimited by comment markers

predicates.title: Structured text manifest block detection
predicates.description: For structured text assets, the validator searches for a C2PA manifest block between designated comment delimiters. If no valid delimiters are found, or only one delimiter is found, the validator reports no manifest found.

input.asset_text: The complete text content of the structured text file

steps.description: Search for the opening and closing C2PA manifest block delimiters in the text
Extract the manifest reference (URL or data: URI) from between the delimiters
Verify the extracted reference is a valid URL or data: URI

passing.description: SVG file with valid C2PA manifest block containing a data: URI

failing_no_block.description: SVG file with no C2PA comment delimiters

failing_empty_reference.description: SVG file with delimiters but empty content between them

failing_malformed_reference.description: SVG file with delimiters containing non-URL content

predicates.title: Single manifest block in structured text
predicates.description: A structured text file must contain at most one C2PA manifest block. Multiple blocks cause rejection.

input.manifest_block_count: Number of C2PA manifest blocks found in the structured text file

predicates.title: Structured text data hash validation
predicates.description: After extracting and resolving the manifest, the structured text asset is validated via the standard data hash mechanism. The manifest block region is the exclusion range.

input.asset_bytes: The complete byte content of the structured text file
input.data_hash_assertion: The DataHashMap from the active manifest

condition.note: Structured text uses standard data hash validation. The manifest block (between delimiters) is treated as the exclusion range.

passing.description: SVG data hash matches after excluding the manifest block

streaming_bmff.description: BMFF streaming and progressive download content validation during playback

predicates.title: Progressive content validation before render
predicates.description: As streaming content becomes available, the validator must validate each portion before it is rendered to the user. Content must not be presented until its hash has been verified.

input.content_portions: Array of content blocks as they arrive during progressive download
input.merkle_tree: The Merkle tree from the BMFF hash assertion

steps.description: Hash the content portion and verify against the corresponding Merkle leaf
Content must be verified before being rendered to the user

on_render_before_verify.note: Validator rendered content before hash verification completed

passing.description: Each video segment is hash-verified before being displayed to the user

failing_premature_render.description: Video segment rendered to user before Merkle leaf verification

predicates.title: Streaming sequence validation
predicates.description: The validator must verify that the sequence of content portions matches the sequence when the manifest was produced. Unexpected discontinuities (not caused by user seek) must be signalled to the user.

input.received_sequence_numbers: Ordered array of sequence numbers as received during playback
input.expected_sequence: Expected monotonically increasing sequence from the manifest

condition.description: Sequence numbers must be monotonically increasing except at user-initiated seek points

on_unexpected_discontinuity.note: Validator must clearly signal to the user that an unexpected discontinuity has occurred

failing_unexpected_gap.reason: Gap from sequence 2 to 10 without user-initiated seek

cross_cutting.description: Predicates that apply across all format families

predicates.title: Hash algorithm in allowed list
predicates.description: The hash algorithm specified in any assertion's alg field must appear in the allowed or deprecated list. This applies to DataHashMap, BmffHashMap, BoxesHashMap, and MultiAssetHashMap alike.

predicates.title: Hard binding assertion presence
predicates.description: If a C2PA Manifest Store is located, the active manifest must contain a hard binding assertion. The validator uses this assertion to validate asset integrity. If no hard binding is found, or it does not validate, the manifest is rejected.

steps.description: Route to format-specific validation based on the hard binding assertion type

predicates.title: Embedded manifest store takes priority over remote
predicates.description: If an asset has both an embedded C2PA Manifest Store and a Link header indicating a remote manifest store, the embedded store takes precedence and the remote reference is ignored.

condition.embedded: C2PA Manifest Store found within the asset container
condition.remote: Link header or XMP provenance pointer to remote location

passing_both_use_embedded.notes: Remote link is ignored when embedded manifest exists

predicates.title: Remote manifest store discovery order
predicates.description: When no embedded manifest store is found, the validator should attempt remote discovery in order: (1) XMP dcterms:provenance in standard asset locations, (2) font C2PA table with activeManifestUriLength, (3) sidecar file at same path with .c2pa extension.

attempts.description: Check asset XMP for dcterms:provenance key with URL
For font assets, check C2PA table with non-zero activeManifestUriLength
Look for file at same path with .c2pa extension

passing_xmp.description: SVG with XMP containing dcterms:provenance pointing to manifest store URL

passing_sidecar.description: JPEG with no embedded manifest but image.jpg.c2pa sidecar file exists

failing_no_remote.description: Asset with no embedded manifest and no discoverable remote location

predicates.title: Compressed manifest decompression
predicates.description: When a compressed manifest (brob box) is encountered, the validator shall decompress it before proceeding. If the decompressed content is not a valid manifest or decompression fails, the manifest is rejected.

steps.description: Check if the manifest is stored in a brob (brotli) compressed box
Verify decompressed content is a valid standard or update manifest

passing.description: Brotli-compressed manifest that decompresses to a valid JUMBF manifest

failing_bad_compression.description: Corrupted brotli data that fails decompression

failing_not_manifest.description: Brotli data that decompresses but is not a valid manifest

predicates.title: Ignore pad and pad2 fields
predicates.description: The validator shall ignore the presence and contents of pad and pad2 fields in all hash map structures (DataHashMap, BmffHashMap, BoxHashMap). These fields are used only for space reservation.

condition.note: pad/pad2 are zero-filled byte strings used for space reservation. They must not affect hash computation or validation logic.

passing.description: DataHashMap with pad field present; validator ignores it during hash computation

failing_if_pad_hashed.description: If a validator incorrectly includes pad bytes in hash computation, the hash will not match

predicates.title: Claim signature within TST validity window
predicates.description: The signing time recorded in the TimeStampToken must fall within the TSA certificate's validity period; if it does the validator emits claimSignature.insideValidity, otherwise the manifest is rejected with claimSignature.outsideValidity.

passing.description: Signing time falls within the TSA certificate validity window

failing_outside_validity.description: Signing time is after the TSA certificate expiry date

predicates.title: Skip or follow timestamp header lookup
predicates.description: If a TimeStampToken was already successfully located and validated from a sigTst or sigTst2 header, the validator must skip the assertion-based timestamp step; otherwise it must follow the assertion-based lookup procedure.

checks.description: A sigTst or sigTst2 header was located

then.description: Timestamp already validated via header; assertion-based step is bypassed

else.description: No valid header timestamp found; proceed to assertion-based timestamp lookup

passing.description: sigTst2 header present and its TimeStampToken passed validation; step is skipped without error

failing_reason.description: No sigTst or sigTst2 header exists; validator must proceed with assertion-based lookup

predicates.title: Manifest identifier lookup in timestamp mapping
predicates.description: When a mapping of C2PA Manifest identifiers to TimeStampTokens exists, the validator must check whether the current manifest's identifier appears in the mapping and, if found, use the associated token (trying each in turn if multiple tokens match) for validation.

steps.description: Check whether a previously built identifier-to-TST mapping exists
Look up the current manifest identifier in the mapping

then.description: Multiple tokens for this identifier; try each until one passes validation, ignore the rest

else.description: Single token found; use it directly for timestamp validation

passing.description: Manifest identifier is in the mapping and the associated TimeStampToken passes validation

failing_reason.description: Multiple tokens found for the identifier but none pass validation

predicates.title: c2pa.time-stamp assertion structural well-formedness
predicates.description: An assertion with label c2pa.time-stamp must consist of well-formed CBOR encoding a single map (Major type 5) containing at least one key/value pair; if this requirement is not met, the claim is rejected with assertion.timestamp.malformed.

steps.description: Applies only when the assertion label is c2pa.time-stamp

passing.description: Assertion label is c2pa.time-stamp and content is a valid CBOR map with at least one entry

failing_reason.description: Assertion content is a CBOR array instead of a map (Major type 4 instead of 5)

predicates.title: Ignore time-stamp manifests inside ingredients
predicates.description: Any time-stamp manifests encountered while processing an ingredient must be ignored and must not participate in timestamp validation of the active manifest.

then.description: Discard any time-stamp manifests found within an ingredient without validation

passing.description: Ingredient contains a time-stamp manifest; validator ignores it and completes without error

failing_reason.description: Validator incorrectly attempts to validate a time-stamp manifest found inside an ingredient, producing an erroneous failure

predicates.title: Display attribution warning for invalid manifest data
predicates.description: When a manifest fails validation, the validator must display a warning that the data lacks validity and must not be attributed to the manifest's signer, and for ingredient manifests must extend that non-attribution warning to the asset's active manifest signer.

checks.description: Warn that the manifest data lacks validity
Warn that data must not be attributed to the manifest's signer

then.description: Also warn that data must not be attributed to the asset's active manifest signer

passing.description: Manifest validation fails and all required warnings are emitted for an ingredient manifest

failing_reason.description: Validator presents invalid manifest data as attributed to the signer without emitting any warning

predicates.title: Gather and validate redacted assertions for each ingredient manifest
predicates.description: The validator must collect redacted assertions for each ingredient's manifest by resolving every JUMBF URI listed in the redacted_assertions field, and a claim's redacted_assertions field must never reference any of the claim's own assertions.

action.description: Check whether the ingredient has a redacted_assertions field

passing.description: Ingredient manifest has redacted_assertions listing URIs from its own prior manifests, none pointing to the claim's own assertions

failing_reason.description: A claim's redacted_assertions field contains a JUMBF URI pointing to one of its own assertions

predicates.title: Validate hashed_uri and hashed_ext_uri references in standard assertions
predicates.description: For all hashed_uri and hashed_ext_uri fields in standard assertions (excluding the activeManifest field in c2pa.ingredient.v3), the validator must perform URI resolution and hash verification; for hashed_ext_uri the validator must additionally follow the external retrieval procedure if it chooses to retrieve the resource.

steps.description: Perform standard hash verification steps

passing.description: All hashed_uri fields resolve correctly and hash values match; hashed_ext_uri external resource also matches when retrieved

failing_reason.description: A hashed_uri in a standard assertion resolves to content whose computed hash does not match the declared hash value

predicates.title: Ingredient nesting with associated manifests
predicates.description: An asset's active manifest may list one or more ingredients via ingredient assertions, and those ingredients may themselves have associated manifests which in turn may contain further nested ingredients and ingredient manifests.

then.description: Ingredient manifests may themselves contain further ingredient nesting

passing.description: Active manifest lists two ingredients, one of which has its own manifest that in turn lists a nested ingredient

failing_reason.description: Ingredient structure is structurally malformed preventing nesting traversal

predicates.title: Execute recursive ingredient validation algorithm
predicates.description: Having compiled the full list of ingredient manifests and all redacted assertions, the validator must execute the specified recursive validation algorithm over the entire ingredient graph.

steps.description: Ingredient manifest list must have been built before this step runs
Redacted assertions list must have been built before this step runs

passing.description: All ingredient manifests pass recursive validation with no redacted assertion conflicts

failing_reason.description: A nested ingredient manifest fails signature verification during recursive validation

predicates.title: Ignore unrecognised manifests not in ingredient list
predicates.description: The validator should ignore any additional C2PA Manifests present in the C2PA Manifest Store that do not appear in the compiled list of ingredient manifests.

then.description: Manifest is not a known ingredient; skip it without error

passing.description: Manifest store contains an extra manifest not referenced by any ingredient; validator ignores it and succeeds

failing_reason.description: Validator attempts to validate an unreferenced manifest and fails because its signature is invalid

predicates.title: Locate hard binding for asset content validation
predicates.description: The asset's content must be validated against a hard binding found in the active manifest (for standard manifests) or resolved by following the chain of parentOf ingredients to the first standard manifest (for update manifests); if no standard manifest with a hard binding is found, the claim is rejected with claim.hardBindings.missing.

else.description: Active manifest is an update manifest; traverse parentOf chain

passing.description: Active manifest is an update manifest; validator follows two parentOf hops to find a standard manifest with a hard binding and validates the asset content successfully

failing_reason.description: Update manifest's parentOf chain contains only update manifests with no terminal standard manifest

predicates.title: Validate BMFF hash excluding update manifest ContentProvenanceBox
predicates.description: The BMFF hash of a manifest's content must be validated per the BMFF-hash procedure, and an appended ContentProvenanceBox with box_purpose set to update should not affect the hash because it was added at the end of the file without shifting any existing offsets.

then.description: ContentProvenanceBox with box_purpose=update was appended and must not alter the BMFF hash calculation

passing.description: BMFF content has an appended ContentProvenanceBox with box_purpose=update; hash validates correctly because the box does not shift existing offsets

failing_reason.description: BMFF content has been tampered and the computed BMFF hash does not match the declared hash value

predicates.title: Validation outcome classification (well-formed / valid / trusted)
predicates.description: A C2PA Manifest may be classified as Well-Formed, Valid, or Trusted, representing successive tiers of validation confidence based on the steps completed.

classification_tiers.description: Structural parsing succeeded; JUMBF box hierarchy is intact and required fields are present
Cryptographic signatures and hard-binding hashes verified successfully
Certificate chain resolves to a trust anchor and credential revocation checks pass

condition.note: Tiers are additive; a manifest cannot be Valid without first being Well-Formed

passing.description: Manifest with intact structure, valid signatures, and trusted certificate chain reaches the Trusted tier

failing_reason.description: Manifest with a missing required JUMBF box cannot advance beyond Well-Formed tier

predicates.title: Custom status code namespace syntax conformance
predicates.description: Any custom status or error code used in validation must conform to the entity-specific reverse-DNS namespace syntax (e.g., com.litware.custom-code).

condition.note: Pattern enforces at least one dot-separated segment consistent with reverse-DNS naming

passing.description: Custom code 'com.litware.provenance-error' satisfies reverse-DNS namespace syntax

failing_reason.description: Code 'myCustomError' with no dot-separated namespace prefix fails syntax validation

predicates.title: PDF update section: single manifest store required
predicates.description: If multiple C2PA Manifest Stores are found within a single PDF update section, all of them must be considered invalid and the validator must behave as if no manifest was located.

passing.description: PDF update section containing exactly one C2PA Manifest Store proceeds to normal validation

failing_reason.description: PDF update section containing two distinct C2PA Manifest Stores causes both to be rejected and treated as absent

predicates.title: Stop manifest search after first located store
predicates.description: Once a C2PA Manifest Store is located in a By Reference lookup, the validator should treat it as the active manifest and stop searching for additional manifest stores.

passing.description: Validator locates one manifest store via the first By Reference location and stops; no further search is performed

failing_reason.description: Validator continues searching after already locating a manifest store, potentially adopting a different store

predicates.title: Remote manifest inaccessibility reporting
predicates.description: When a manifest is documented to exist at a remote location but cannot be retrieved - either because the resource is absent or the location is currently unavailable - the validator must emit manifest.inaccessible; if no manifest store is found via standard paths, the validator may search additional locations before concluding inaccessibility.

passing.description: Remote manifest URL returns a valid C2PA Manifest Store successfully

failing_reason.description: Remote manifest URL documented in the asset returns HTTP 404; validator emits manifest.inaccessible

predicates.title: HTTP Link header manifest discovery and childlabel exclusion
predicates.description: When an asset is retrieved over HTTP, the validator should check the response Link header for a rel=c2pa-manifest parameter to locate the manifest store; if found, any childlabel fragment in the JUMBF URI must be ignored because references to specific manifests within the store are not permitted.

then.note: childlabel portion is silently ignored; not an error

passing.description: HTTP response includes Link header with rel=c2pa-manifest pointing to a valid manifest store URL, no childlabel present

failing_reason.description: JUMBF URI contains a childlabel fragment; validator must strip it and not treat the specific manifest reference as valid

predicates.title: Optional manifest-asset association verification
predicates.description: A validator may optionally verify that the located C2PA Manifest Store is the one actually associated with the asset under inspection, as a guard against manifest substitution.

passing.description: Manifest store binding confirms association with the asset being validated

failing_reason.description: Manifest store binding references a different asset identifier than the one being inspected

predicates.title: Claim required fields and claim_generator_info name presence
predicates.description: The claim must contain all required fields, and the claim_generator_info entry must include a name field; if any required field is absent the claim must be rejected with claim.malformed.

steps.note: Every entry in claim_generator_info must have a name field

passing.description: Claim contains all required fields and each claim_generator_info entry includes a name field

failing_reason.description: claim_generator_info entry is present but lacks a name field; validator rejects the claim with claim.malformed

predicates.title: Generator-info icon field structural validation
predicates.description: If a generator-info-map referenced by the claim_generator_info field of a claim-map or claim-map-v2 contains an icon field, the validator must validate that icon value according to the icon validation rules.

passing.description: claim_generator_info entry with a valid hashed URI icon field passes icon validation

failing_reason.description: claim_generator_info icon field contains a malformed URI that fails icon validation rules

predicates.title: CA certificate revocation via AIA OCSP
predicates.description: For CA certificates in the chain, the validator should determine revocation status using the Authority Information Access extension, and should prefer OCSP responses already present in the C2PA Manifest when the AIA extension indicates OCSP is available.

then.note: Prefer stapled responses before going online

passing.description: CA certificate AIA extension lists an OCSP URL; manifest contains a stapled OCSP response confirming the CA cert is valid

failing_reason.description: CA certificate AIA extension lists OCSP but neither stapled responses nor online OCSP are available, leaving revocation status unknown

predicates.title: Signer certificate revocation validation process
predicates.description: For the signer's certificate, the validator must follow the full revocation validation process: if the certificate provides no revocation method it is treated as not revoked, and if the claim generator stapled OCSP responses in the rVals header the validator must decode and validate those responses.

passing.description: Signer certificate contains no AIA extension; validator treats the credential as not revoked and proceeds

failing_reason.description: COSE_Sign1 rVals header contains an OCSP response that fails structural validation

predicates.title: Multiple OCSP responses: try each until one passes
predicates.description: If more than one OCSP response is found for a certificate, the validator must attempt each in turn and stop as soon as one successfully passes validation, ignoring the remaining responses.

passing.description: Two OCSP responses present; the first fails validation but the second passes, so the certificate is treated as valid

failing_reason.description: Three OCSP responses present for the certificate; all three fail structural validation

predicates.title: Online OCSP fallback when no revocation info in manifest
predicates.description: If the C2PA Manifest Store contains no revocation information for the certificate and the validator is online, the validator should query the OCSP responder directly to determine revocation status.

then.note: URL sourced from the certificate AIA extension

passing.description: No OCSP data in manifest; validator is online and successfully queries the OCSP responder, receiving a good status

failing_reason.description: No OCSP data in manifest; validator is online but the OCSP responder returns a revoked status for the certificate

predicates.title: OCSP revoked certStatus disambiguation and rejection
predicates.description: When an OCSP response in the manifest has certStatus of revoked, the validator must inspect the revocationReason to distinguish removedFromCRL (not a true revocation) from an actual revocation; a genuine revocation must cause the claim to be rejected with signingCredential.ocsp.revoked.

passing.description: OCSP response certStatus is revoked but revocationReason is removeFromCRL; certificate is treated as not revoked

failing_reason.description: OCSP response certStatus is revoked with revocationReason keyCompromise; claim rejected with signingCredential.ocsp.revoked

predicates.title: OCSP skipped and OCSP inaccessible informational codes
predicates.description: If the validator opts not to perform an online OCSP check it must emit signingCredential.ocsp.skipped; if it attempts an online OCSP query but receives no response it must emit signingCredential.ocsp.inaccessible.

passing.description: Validator configured to skip online OCSP; emits signingCredential.ocsp.skipped informational code and does not fail the claim

failing_reason.description: Validator attempts online OCSP query but network is unavailable; emits signingCredential.ocsp.inaccessible but does not by itself fail the claim

predicates.title: Online OCSP certStatus unknown/revoked outcome handling
predicates.description: When an online OCSP response returns certStatus unknown the validator must record signingCredential.ocsp.unknown; when the status is revoked the claim must be rejected with signingCredential.ocsp.revoked.

passing.description: Online OCSP response returns certStatus good; certificate considered valid and claim proceeds

failing_reason.description: Online OCSP response returns certStatus revoked; claim rejected with signingCredential.ocsp.revoked

predicates.title: Forward-compatible c2pa.metadata assertion field tolerance
predicates.description: Validators should not reject c2pa.metadata assertions that contain fields outside the currently defined allowed list, in order to preserve forward compatibility as the allowed list evolves.

then.note: Do not fail; continue validation of remaining known fields

passing.description: c2pa.metadata assertion contains one standard field and one unrecognised field; validator accepts the assertion and continues

failing_reason.description: Validator incorrectly rejects a c2pa.metadata assertion solely because it contains an unrecognised field not yet in the allowed list

predicates.title: Hashed URI field presence and destination reachability
predicates.description: If a hashed URI field is absent or the data at its referenced destination cannot be located, the validator must treat this as a validation failure with code hashedURI.missing.

passing.description: Hashed URI field is present and the data at the referenced JUMBF location is successfully resolved

failing_reason.description: Hashed URI field references a JUMBF box label that does not exist in the manifest; validator emits hashedURI.missing

predicates.title: JPEG APP11 exclusion range total length matches manifest store length
predicates.description: For JPEG 1 files, the validator must confirm that the total byte length of all APP11 segments representing the C2PA Manifest Store equals the total length declared in the exclusion range, to detect tampering with segment length fields.

passing.description: Sum of all APP11 segment lengths equals the total declared exclusion range length for the manifest store region

failing_reason.description: One APP11 segment length header was modified after signing; the sum no longer matches the declared exclusion range total, emitting assertion.dataHash.mismatch

predicates.title: Optional validation result metadata fields
predicates.description: Validation results may include a SemVer-formatted specVersion field identifying the specification version used and a trustListURI field identifying the trust list used for signature validation.

passing.description: Validation result contains specVersion '2.1.0' and a valid trustListURI pointing to the C2PA trust list

passing_absent.description: Validation result omits both specVersion and trustListURI fields entirely

failing_reason.description: Validation result contains specVersion '2.1' which is not SemVer-formatted (missing patch segment)

predicates.title: Claim signature URI resolution
predicates.description: The validator must retrieve the COSE signature by resolving the URI in the claim's signature field, and must reject the claim if the field is absent, the URI is unresolvable, or the URI resolves outside the same C2PA Manifest box.

steps.description: Verify the resolved URI points to a location within the same C2PA Manifest box as the claim
URI resolved to a COSE signature within the same manifest box

passing.description: Claim's signature field contains a valid relative URI that resolves to a COSE_Sign1 structure within the same C2PA Manifest box

failing_unresolvable.description: Claim's signature field contains a URI that cannot be resolved within the manifest store

failing_cross_manifest.description: Claim's signature field URI resolves to a COSE structure in a different C2PA Manifest box than the claim

predicates.title: Signing credential validation
predicates.description: The signing credential embedded in or referenced by the COSE signature must be validated according to its credential type requirements, and the claim must be rejected if the credential is not acceptable.

steps.description: Validate the signing credential per the requirements of its credential type (X.509 for standard C2PA manifests)
Credential is acceptable per its type requirements

passing.description: COSE signature contains a valid X.509 certificate chain where the end-entity certificate has the correct key usage and is within its validity period

failing_reason.description: End-entity certificate in the x5chain has an expired validity period and does not have a valid time-stamp to anchor the signing time

predicates.title: Signature algorithm allowed list check
predicates.description: The signature algorithm used in the COSE signature must appear on the C2PA allowed or deprecated algorithm list, and the claim must be rejected if the algorithm is unsupported.

passing.description: COSE signature uses ES256 (ECDSA with P-256 and SHA-256), which is on the allowed list

passing_deprecated.description: COSE signature uses RS256, which is on the deprecated list and still accepted with a warning

failing_reason.description: COSE signature uses a custom or proprietary algorithm identifier not present on either the allowed or deprecated list

predicates.title: Timestamp presence check before trust chain verification
predicates.description: Before verifying a chain of trust to a trust anchor, the validator must determine whether a valid time-stamp is present, and should then verify the credential chain from the signing credential to a trust anchor in an applicable trust anchor list.

steps.description: Determine whether a sigTst or sigTst2 time-stamp token is present in the COSE signature header

on_absent.note: No time-stamp present; validation continues using current time for certificate validity

on_present.note: Time-stamp present; validate per PRED-CRYP-011 through PRED-CRYP-017 before using attested time

steps.description: Verify chain of trust from signing credential to applicable trust anchor list

passing.description: Claim signature contains a sigTst time-stamp token; validator determines its presence before proceeding to trust chain verification

passing_no_tst.description: Claim signature has no time-stamp token; validator proceeds to trust chain verification using current time

failing_reason.description: Validator skips the timestamp check step and proceeds directly to trust anchor lookup, potentially using wrong validity time for certificate checking

predicates.title: Trust anchor temporal validity gating
predicates.description: A trust anchor configuration with a notBefore date must not validate signatures whose attested or current time is before that date, and a configuration with a notAfter date must not validate signatures whose attested or current time is after that date.

passing.description: Signing time of 2024-06-01 falls within the trust anchor's notBefore of 2024-01-01 and notAfter of 2025-01-01

failing_before_notbefore.description: Signing time of 2023-12-31 is before the trust anchor's notBefore of 2024-01-01

failing_after_notafter.description: Signing time of 2025-06-01 is after the trust anchor's notAfter of 2025-01-01

predicates.title: Certificate chain of trust to trust anchor
predicates.description: If a valid chain of trust from the signing credential to a recognized trust anchor cannot be established, the claim must be rejected; otherwise the credential is assigned a trusted status.

passing.description: End-entity certificate chains through an intermediate CA to a root CA present in the C2PA Trust List; chain is complete and all signatures valid

failing_reason.description: Intermediate CA certificate is missing from the x5chain and cannot be retrieved; chain is incomplete and trust cannot be established

predicates.title: COSE claim signature cryptographic verification
predicates.description: The validator must cryptographically verify the COSE_Sign1 signature over the claim bytes; a failed verification yields claimSignature.mismatch and a successful verification yields claimSignature.validated.

steps.description: Confirm claim has not already been rejected by prior validation steps
Verify the COSE_Sign1 signature over the Sig_Structure encoding of the claim

passing.description: COSE_Sign1 signature over the claim CBOR bytes verifies successfully with the signing certificate's public key

failing_tampered_claim.description: Claim CBOR bytes were modified after signing; COSE signature verification fails

failing_wrong_key.description: The public key in the x5chain does not correspond to the private key used to create the COSE signature

predicates.title: COSE header bucket placement permissiveness
predicates.description: Unless the specification explicitly restricts a COSE header to either the protected or unprotected bucket, a header may appear in either bucket.

then.description: Enforce bucket placement only for headers explicitly restricted by the specification

else.description: No bucket restriction applies; accept the header in either bucket

passing.description: A content type header appears in the protected bucket; no spec restriction mandates one bucket for this header

passing_unprotected.description: The same content type header appears in the unprotected bucket; still accepted

failing_reason.description: A header explicitly required to be in the protected bucket by the specification appears only in the unprotected bucket

predicates.title: Multiple tstToken entries rejected as malformed
predicates.description: If the COSE signature unprotected header contains more than one tstToken entry, the validator must issue a timeStamp.malformed informational code and ignore all time-stamps in that header.

passing.description: COSE signature unprotected header contains exactly one tstToken entry

failing_reason.description: COSE signature unprotected header contains two tstToken entries

predicates.title: sigTst time-stamp response PKI status validation
predicates.description: A validator supporting sigTst must retrieve the RFC 3161 TimeStampResp from the tstToken's val property and reject the time-stamp as malformed unless the PKIStatusInfo status field is 0 (granted) or 1 (grantedWithMods).

steps.description: PKI status is granted or grantedWithMods; continue with TimeStampToken validation

passing.description: sigTst tstToken.val is a valid RFC 3161 TimeStampResp with PKIStatusInfo status of 0 (granted)

passing_granted_with_mods.description: sigTst tstToken.val is a valid RFC 3161 TimeStampResp with PKIStatusInfo status of 1 (grantedWithMods)

failing_reason.description: sigTst tstToken.val is a valid RFC 3161 TimeStampResp with PKIStatusInfo status of 2 (rejection)

predicates.title: sigTst2 TimeStampToken retrieval
predicates.description: A validator supporting sigTst2 must retrieve the tstToken's val property and confirm it is an RFC 3161 TimeStampToken (TST) before proceeding with time-stamp validation.

steps.description: val is a valid RFC 3161 TimeStampToken; continue with TST validation

passing.description: sigTst2 tstToken.val is a well-formed RFC 3161 TimeStampToken DER-encoded structure

failing_wrong_format.description: sigTst2 tstToken.val contains a full TimeStampResp (response wrapper) instead of a bare TimeStampToken

failing_reason.description: sigTst2 tstToken.val field is absent from the tstToken object

predicates.title: TimeStampToken signature and messageImprint validation
predicates.description: The validator must reject a time-stamp whose algorithm is unsupported, whose signature does not verify, whose messageImprint field is absent, or whose message imprint hash algorithm is unsupported or whose computed hash does not match the covered data.

steps.description: Validate the CMS signature within the timeStampToken per RFC 5652 Section 5.6
For sigTst: hash the bytes of COSE_Sign1_Tagged. For sigTst2: hash the signature bytes from the COSE structure.

passing.description: TimeStampToken uses SHA-256 for both the signature digest and message imprint, signature verifies, and the message imprint matches the hash of the COSE_Sign1_Tagged bytes

failing_bad_algorithm.description: TimeStampToken uses MD5 as its signature digest algorithm, which is not on the allowed or deprecated list

failing_bad_signature.description: TimeStampToken CMS signature does not verify against the TSA certificate's public key

failing_missing_imprint.description: TimeStampToken tspInfo structure does not contain a messageImprint field

failing_reason.description: Message imprint hash in the TimeStampToken does not match the SHA-256 hash of the COSE_Sign1_Tagged bytes

predicates.title: TimeStampToken TSA certificate chain validation
predicates.description: The TimeStampToken must contain a certificates field from which the TSA signing certificate can be located and a trust chain built to an entry in the C2PA TSA Trust List; if either the certificate cannot be found or the chain fails, the time-stamp is rejected as untrusted.

steps.description: Locate the TSA signing certificate within the provided certificates field

passing.description: TimeStampToken certificates field contains the TSA certificate and an intermediate CA, both forming a complete chain to a root in the C2PA TSA Trust List

failing_no_certs_field.description: TimeStampToken does not include a certificates field

failing_reason.description: The TSA signing certificate is present in the certificates field but the issuing CA is not in the C2PA TSA Trust List and no intermediate can bridge to a trusted root

predicates.title: TSA certificate temporal validity and credentialInvalid informational code
predicates.description: If the validator determines a TSA certificate is invalid it may return a timeStamp.credentialInvalid informational code, and should additionally confirm that the genTime attested time falls within the validity period of the TSA certificate chain up to the trust anchor.

if.description: TSA certificate failed some validity check

steps.description: genTime must fall within the notBefore/notAfter range of every certificate in the TSA chain up to the trust anchor

passing.description: genTime of 2024-06-15T12:00:00Z falls within the validity period of both the TSA signing certificate and its issuing CA

failing_reason.description: genTime of 2024-06-15T12:00:00Z is after the TSA signing certificate's notAfter of 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z

predicates.title: Time-stamp and signing certificate validity window enforcement
predicates.description: If all time-stamp validation steps pass and both timeStamp.trusted and timeStamp.validated codes are issued, the validator must confirm the signing certificate was valid at the attested time; if it was not, the claim must be rejected with claimSignature.outsideValidity.

steps.description: Confirm that no prior timestamp step caused a stop or failure condition
Issue timeStamp.trusted and timeStamp.validated success codes
Confirm the attested time falls within the signing certificate's notBefore/notAfter range
Signing certificate was valid at the attested time; proceed to signing CA chain validity check

passing.description: TSA attested time of 2024-06-15 falls within the signing certificate's validity period of 2024-01-01 through 2025-01-01; all timestamp codes issued successfully

failing_reason.description: TSA attested time of 2023-12-01 is before the signing certificate's notBefore of 2024-01-01; signing cert was not yet valid at claimed signing time

predicates.title: Use attested time for certificate validity when trusted timestamp present
predicates.description: When a time-stamp is present, trusted, and validated, the validator must use the TSA-attested time rather than the current wall-clock time when determining the time validity of the signing certificate and the TSA certificate.

then.description: Use genTime as the reference time for all certificate validity period checks

else.description: No trusted timestamp available; use current time for certificate validity checks

passing.description: Trusted and validated timestamp present with genTime of 2024-06-15; validator uses 2024-06-15 rather than current date 2026-04-07 for certificate validity checks

failing_reason.description: Trusted and validated timestamp present but validator uses current time 2026-04-07 for checking certificate validity; signing certificate had notAfter of 2024-12-31 and would be considered expired

predicates.title: Claimed time of signing via iat header validation
predicates.description: A validator may validate the claimed time of signing from the iat protected header, optionally confirming it falls within the certificate chain validity period and is not later than the attested time from any associated trusted time-stamp.

passing.description: iat header of 2024-06-15 falls within the signing certificate's validity period of 2024-01-01 through 2025-01-01 and is not later than the trusted timestamp's genTime of 2024-06-15

passing_no_iat.description: No iat header present in the COSE protected header; validation step is skipped

failing_reason.description: iat header claims signing time of 2023-12-01 but the signing certificate's notBefore is 2024-01-01; claimed time is before certificate was valid

predicates.title: Claimed time of signing inside/outside validity informational code
predicates.description: If the validator checks the iat claimed time against the certificate chain validity period, it must return timeOfSigning.insideValidity when the time falls within the period and timeOfSigning.outsideValidity when it does not.

passing.description: Claimed signing time from iat header falls within the full certificate chain's validity periods; timeOfSigning.insideValidity is returned

failing_reason.description: Claimed signing time from iat header is one day before the signing certificate's notBefore date; timeOfSigning.outsideValidity is returned

predicates.title: CA certificate revocation check at signing time
predicates.description: If the validator determines a CA certificate in the chain was revoked at the time indicated by a trusted time-stamp, or at current time if no trusted time-stamp is present, the claim signature must be rejected as untrusted.

passing.description: All CA certificates in the signing chain were not revoked at the trusted timestamp's attested signing time of 2024-06-15

failing_reason.description: The intermediate CA certificate was revoked on 2024-06-01 and the trusted timestamp attests to a signing time of 2024-06-10, which is after the revocation date

predicates.title: OCSP response from manifest store revocation check
predicates.description: The validator must decode OCSP responses embedded in the C2PA Manifest Store per RFC requirements, and if any response satisfies the conditions for the certificate of interest, must either issue signingCredential.ocsp.notRevoked or perform an additional online OCSP check.

steps.description: Decode each OCSP response in the manifest store per RFC 5019 section 3.2 requirements 1-4

if.description: At least one decoded OCSP response in the manifest store satisfies all conditions for the certificate being checked

options.description: Perform additional online OCSP check for higher assurance

passing.description: C2PA Manifest Store contains a valid OCSP response for the signing certificate confirming not-revoked status at the time of signing; signingCredential.ocsp.notRevoked is issued

passing_no_ocsp.description: C2PA Manifest Store contains no OCSP responses; validator proceeds to online OCSP check

failing_reason.description: OCSP response in the manifest store is malformed and fails RFC 5019 section 3.2 requirement 2 (response signature verification); certificate is treated as unconfirmed

predicates.title: Online OCSP query fallback
predicates.description: If no embedded OCSP response satisfies the conditions for a given certificate, or if the claim signature lacks a time-stamp, the validator may query the OCSP responder online using the accessLocation found in the certificate's Authority Information Access extension.

checks.description: No OCSP response in manifest store satisfied conditions for this certificate
Claim signature does not have a time-stamp

then.description: Query OCSP responder at the accessLocation URI from the certificate's AIA extension per RFC 6960

passing.description: No manifest-store OCSP response is available; validator queries the OCSP responder at the URI from the certificate's AIA extension and receives a valid good response

passing_no_query.description: Validator chooses not to perform an online OCSP query even though no manifest-store response is available; this is acceptable as the check is optional

failing_reason.description: Online OCSP response indicates the certificate was revoked prior to the signing time; revocation is confirmed

predicates.title: Online OCSP response acceptance and not-revoked determination
predicates.description: An online OCSP response accepted per RFC requirements establishes not-revoked status if certStatus is good, or if certStatus is revoked with a non-removeFromCRL reason and revocation occurred after the signing time, triggering a signingCredential.ocsp.notRevoked code.

on_not_accepted.description: Response does not meet acceptance requirements; cannot establish revocation status

steps.description: Check certStatus to determine not-revoked outcome

checks.description: Revocation occurred after the time of signing

passing_good.description: Online OCSP response returns certStatus good for the signing certificate; signingCredential.ocsp.notRevoked is issued

passing_post_signing_revocation.description: certStatus is revoked with reason keyCompromise, but revocation time of 2024-09-01 is after the trusted signing time of 2024-06-15; certificate was valid at time of signing

failing_removeFromCRL.description: certStatus is revoked with reason removeFromCRL; the not-revoked exception does not apply and the certificate is treated as revoked

failing_reason.description: certStatus is revoked with reason keyCompromise and revocation time of 2024-05-01 is before the signing time of 2024-06-15; certificate was revoked at signing time

predicates.title: c2pa.session-keys signerBinding signature verification
predicates.description: When an assertion has the label c2pa.session-keys, the validator must verify that the signerBinding signature is correct, confirming the signer possesses the session key private key.

steps.description: Verify the signerBinding signature to confirm the signer knows the session key private key
signerBinding signature is valid; session key ownership confirmed

passing.description: c2pa.session-keys assertion is present with a signerBinding whose signature verifies correctly, confirming the signer possesses the session key private key

failing_invalid_binding.description: c2pa.session-keys assertion is present but the signerBinding signature does not verify; the private key is not known to the signer

failing_reason.description: c2pa.session-keys assertion is present but the signerBinding field is absent from the assertion data

predicates.title: No hash mismatch codes for activeManifest field in claim signature hash validation
predicates.description: When using the claim signature hash validation method, the validator must not record hash mismatch failure codes for the activeManifest field, as this field is excluded from the hash computation by design.

if.description: Claim signature hash validation method is in use

checks.description: The activeManifest field must not be included in hash mismatch tracking during claim signature hash validation

on_violation.description: Validator incorrectly recorded a hash mismatch for the activeManifest field

passing.description: Claim signature hash validation method is used; validator computes and checks hashes but does not emit any hash mismatch code for the activeManifest field

failing_reason.description: Validator using the claim signature hash validation method emits a hash mismatch failure code for the activeManifest field, which is explicitly excluded from this check

predicates.title: Ingredient validation and results recording
predicates.description: When a claim generator adds an ingredient, it must act as a validator, run the full validation algorithm on the ingredient, and record the results in the validationResults field of the ingredient assertion.

steps.description: Run full validation algorithm on the ingredient asset

passing.description: Ingredient assertion includes a populated validationResults field reflecting a valid ingredient

failing_reason.description: Ingredient assertion is present but validationResults field is absent

predicates.title: Multiple embedded manifest stores invalidation
predicates.description: If more than one C2PA Manifest Store is embedded in an asset, all are treated as invalid and no manifests are considered located; additionally, none of the embedded manifests shall be included in any ingredient assertion.

on_true.description: Treat all stores as invalid; no manifests located

steps.description: Ensure no embedded C2PA manifests are referenced in the ingredient assertion when multiple stores are present

passing.description: Asset contains exactly one embedded C2PA Manifest Store

failing_reason.description: Asset contains two distinct embedded C2PA Manifest Stores

predicates.title: Redacted assertion label check
predicates.description: For each assertion, the validator must check whether its URI is in the redacted list; if it is and its label is c2pa.actions or c2pa.actions.v2, the claim must be rejected with assertion.action.redacted.

passing.description: Assertion URI is not in the redacted list and label is c2pa.thumbnail

failing_reason.description: Assertion URI is in the redacted list and label is c2pa.actions

predicates.title: Assertion URI must reference same manifest
predicates.description: If the assertion URI does not refer to a location within the same C2PA Manifest (a self#jumbf location), the claim must be rejected with assertion.outsideManifest.

passing.description: Assertion URL begins with self#jumbf= pointing into the current manifest
